title: Sorbus aria subsp. edulis
what_description: Sorbus aria subsp. edulis is a subspecies of Sorbus aria, a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with a rounded crown, growing up to 8 meters tall, and characterized by its simple, alternate leaves, which are 4-9 cm long, pinnate and have 11-23 leaflets. The leaves are dark green on the upper surface and pale green on the lower surface, with serrated margins and a cuneate base. The plant produces small, white or pink flowers in May, in corymbose panicles, and red or yellowish berries in autumn, which are edible. The plant's bark is greyish-brown, smooth and exfoliating, with horizontal lenticels.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Sorbus aria subsp. edulis are inflammation, diarrhea, gastroenteritis and digestive issues, caused by the anthraquinones present in the plant which have anti-inflammatory properties. Inflammation and diarrhea can be reduced with the help of a compound called sorbitol, which decreases water absorption in the intestines and reduces bowel movements. Rheumatism and arthritis can be alleviated due to the presence of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. Respiratory issues and coughs can be treated with the help of the plant's flavonoids, which also have expectorant properties that help relieve congestion. Skin irritation and eczema can be healed with the help of the plant's anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can reduce redness and itching of the skin.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Sorbus aria subsp. edulis are attributed to its antioxidant capacity, which is largely due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress. Its anti-inflammatory properties are also notable, with saponins and triterpenoids contributing to the reduction of inflammation and alleviation of symptoms associated with various conditions. Furthermore, the antimicrobial properties of Sorbus aria subsp. edulis have been linked to the presence of volatile oils and saponins, which exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, thereby preventing infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, the analgesic properties of this plant are attributed to the presence of terpenoids and phenolic compounds, which modulate pain perception and alleviate discomfort. Its hepatoprotective properties are also significant, with flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibiting antioxidant and anti-apoptotic activities that protect the liver from damage and promote its regeneration.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Sorbus aria subsp. edulis are flavonoids, phenolic acids, tannins, quercetin, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties due to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Flavonoids, specifically quercetin, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in various studies. Phenolic acids and tannins also contribute to the plant's antioxidant capacity, while quercetin has been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms. Saponins in Sorbus aria subsp. edulis have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in wound healing and skin protection. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Sorbus aria subsp. edulis provides a comprehensive range of health benefits, supporting its traditional use in folk medicine.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Sorbus aria subsp. edulis are tea, tincture, infusion, salve, and infused oil. The tea is prepared by steeping dried flowers, leaves, or fruits in hot water to utilize its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties for digestive issues and wound healing. The tincture is made by soaking the dried plant material in a solvent to create a concentrated liquid extract used for treating colds, fever, and respiratory infections. The infusion is a weaker liquid extract prepared by steeping the dried plant material in cold water, often used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in treating skin conditions and wounds. The salve and infused oil are topical preparations used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation by applying the extracted plant material to the affected area.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Sorbus aria subsp. edulis improperly are causing diarrhea due to the high tannin content, inducing vomiting and nausea as a result of the bitter compounds, increasing heart rate due to the stimulant properties of certain alkaloids, and developing allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. These effects are attributed to the plant's toxic compounds, including amygdalin, which can release cyanide, a potent toxin, when ingested. The improper use of Sorbus aria subsp. edulis can also lead to cardiac issues, as the stimulant properties can exacerbate pre-existing heart conditions. Furthermore, the high concentration of tannins can interfere with nutrient absorption, potentially causing gastrointestinal upset. If ingested in large quantities or for an extended period, Sorbus aria subsp. edulis can cause serious health complications.
